channel.title: Geoff Dixon > Write-Verbose
channel.description: Recent content on Geoff Dixon > Write-Verbose

item.title: Quick steps to enable WSL2 on Windows 10
item.description: A quick summary of the manual steps to enable WSL2

item.title: Neo4J CMDB pt1 - MEMCM Export
item.description:
This is the first post in a series on creating a graph database CMDB.

In part one we’ll cover exporting computer information from Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager (a.k.a SCCM) using PowerShell and T-SQL. MEMCM is one datasource used to create a configuration management database (CMDB) using a Neo4j graph database.

item.title: Custom Snippets in VSCode
item.description:
Background

Snippets are a great productivity feature in VSCode that allow common code blocks to be defined as templates.

item.title: Find a computer's Active Directory Site and Subnet with PowerShell
item.description: Read-on for a PowerShell command to get the Active Directory Subnet and Site from the computername or IP Address.

item.title: Export Remote Eventlog with PowerShell
item.description: This article includes a PowerShell Export-Eventlog command to quickly export a Windows event log from a remote computer and copy it to the local machine.

item.title: Active Directory ADSystemInfo with PowerShell
item.description: ADSystemInfo is a built-in COM object in Windows 7, 8.1 and 10 that simplifies lookup of Active Directory user and computer information.

item.title: Get direct and nested Active Directory group membership with PowerShell
item.description: This post lists various methods to get all the groups a Windows user is a member of (direct and through group nesting)

item.title: Config Manager 'Potential SQL performance issue caused by change tracking retention period'
item.description: If you come across this warning during a Config Manager Current Branch site upgrade, read on for the solution.

item.title: DIY Wireless Suppression with WMI and PowerShell
item.description:
When you drop a laptop into a docking station with an Ethernet cable, you expect it to use the wired connection instead of than WIFI. This may not be the case on Windows unless wireless is supressed.

This post will cover the problem, common 3rd-party solutions and a built-in solution using WMI and PowerShell

item.title: PowerShell Toolmaking via RegEx
item.description: This post shows how to create a PowerShell Get-WLAN function, parsing the output of the built-in Windows NETSH.EXE using a Regular Expression

item.title: Audit Chrome Extensions with PowerShell
item.description: This post includes a PowerShell Get-ChromeExtension script to list installed extensions on the local or remote computer.

item.title: PSPam - Privileged Access Management via PowerShell
item.description:
Update 2019-01-12: A Microsoft security update appears to have broken this solution.

TL;DR the following describes a DIY method for granualar assignment of elevated local permissions on Windows, using PowerShell Just Enough Administration (JEA)
